join_order.build: sub(fy=2022 qualifying adsh codes — compact index, 16890 entries)
join_order.notes: Pre-build int16_t adsh_compact_idx[86135]: maps adsh_code to compact_index 0..16889 (or -1 if fy!=2022). During scan, single array lookup replaces fy check + cik/name lookup + 2 hash inserts. Sub columns only needed for building compact index and post-merge aggregation — not in hot scan loop.

filters.notes: Load usd_code at runtime from shared/uom.dict by matching string 'USD'. Never hardcode.
IEEE 754 NaN sentinel for NULL. Applied together with uom filter.
fy=2022 filter is encoded in adsh_compact_idx at pre-processing time. No sub_fy array lookup in hot scan loop — just a single int16_t array read.

data_structures.adsh_compact_idx: int16_t[86135] shared read-only array — 172KB, L2-resident in each core. Maps adsh_code -> compact_index (0..16889) or -1. Built once pre-scan from sub/fy.bin scan.
data_structures.compact_cik: int32_t[16890] — cik for each qualifying adsh code (built at pre-processing)
data_structures.compact_name: int32_t[16890] — name_code for each qualifying adsh code (built at pre-processing)
data_structures.thread_local_compact_sum: double[16890] per thread — 131KB per thread, FITS IN L2 (256KB). 64 threads x 131KB = 8.4MB total, well within L3 (44MB). Zero-initialized. Replaces both cik_ht and nc_ht.
data_structures.post_merge_cik_map: compact_hash_map(int32_t cik -> double sum), pre-sized 16384 slots — built after merge over only 16890 entries
data_structures.post_merge_nc_map: compact_hash_map(int64_t (name_code<<32|cik) -> double sum), pre-sized 32768 slots — built after merge over only 16890 entries
data_structures.name_dict: std::vector<std::string> loaded upfront during data_loading — eliminates separate decode_name_codes phase

parallelism.thread_local_state: each thread owns independent double[16890] compact_sum array (131KB); direct indexed write, no hash computation, no probing, no synchronization during scan
parallelism.merge_phase: parallel reduction: #pragma omp parallel for over compact_index 0..16889; each thread sums tl_compact_sum[t][ci] across all nthreads into global_compact_sum[ci]. Total merge data: 8.6MB (vs 72MB in iter_0). Post-merge: single sequential loop over 16890 entries builds small cik and nc hash maps.

indexes.notes: Read indexes/num_zonemaps.bin. Pre-compute first and last USD block indices before the parallel scan. Skip blocks where uom_min > usd_code OR uom_max < usd_code. Reduces scan range to USD segment only.

aggregation: two_phase: (1) parallel flat-array accumulation by compact adsh_code (L2-resident, no hashing); (2) sequential post-merge hash_group_by over 16890 entries for cik and (name,cik) keys

output.decode_step: name_dict already loaded upfront during data_loading; index directly by name_code for surviving groups

optimization_notes: Root cause is Category B (algorithm change): the thread-local hash maps in iter_0 use long double slots (24 bytes each), making the combined thread-local working set 64 x (393KB + 786KB) = 72MB — far exceeding L3 (44MB). This causes the merge phase to scan 72MB of cache-cold data (observed: 27.5ms). Fix: replace both hash maps with a single thread-local flat array double[16890] per thread (131KB), indexed by a pre-built compact adsh_code index that encodes the fy=2022 filter. This reduces per-thread working set to 131KB (fits in L2 per core), total merge data to 8.6MB (fits easily in L3), and eliminates hash computation + probing from the hot scan path. The scan loop simplifies to: one int16_t array lookup + one double array write per qualifying row (vs fy lookup + cik/name lookup + 2 hash inserts). Post-merge aggregation into cik and (name,cik) hash maps is trivial (16890 entries). Additionally, sub/name.dict is loaded upfront during data_loading to eliminate the separate 6.7ms decode_name_codes phase.
